items.title: Understanding the Relationship Between the Big Five Personality Traits and the Cognitive Appraisals Leading to Emotions: An Integrative Narrative Review
items.author: Livia Sacchi, Elise Dan-Glauser
items.content: <article>Emotion Review, Ahead of Print. In the Component Process Model (CPM), emotion is defined as a rapid psychological process whose emergence relies on appraisals (i.e., cognitive evaluations) of the environment. Personality encompasses many affective components and traits thought to shape ...</article>

items.title: Ecological Grief as a Shared Emotion
items.author: Pablo Fernandez Velasco, Louise Richardson
items.content: <article>Emotion Review, Ahead of Print. There is a growing interdisciplinary effort to understand the emotional dimensions of the climate crisis. A central focus in this line of research is ecological grief—the grief felt in relation to ecological losses. Ecological grief is frequently claimed ...</article>
